concept.conceptName: Interleukin 6 | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge

en.name: Interleukin-6 (IL-6) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Interleukin-6 (IL-6) is a pro-inflammatory cytokine secreted by macrophages, T cells and endothelial cells in response to infection or tissue injury. It is one of the earliest cytokines to rise in sepsis (peaks within a few hours, much earlier than CRP) and drives the hepatic acute-phase response — in particular CRP and serum amyloid A production [1]. In intensive care, IL-6 is used as an early sepsis marker and to monitor cytokine release syndrome (CRS) in CAR-T therapy and severe COVID-19 [2]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by immunoassay. Results are reported as a mass concentration in pg/mL (most common in clinical practice) or as an enzyme-activity concentration in U/mL (less common) [3]. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the LOINC Hierarchy `Interleukin 6 | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge`, expanded to include all its descendants. The set resolves to two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts: - *3023091 / 26881-3 — Interleukin 6 [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3009104 / 4655-7 — Interleukin 6 [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: enzyme-activity-style unit, rarely used. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`pg/mL`** (concept 8806 — picogram per millilitre) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concept in Serum or Plasma is deliberately excluded by name: - *3044155 / 44322-6 — Interleukin 6 [Presence] in Serum or Plasma*: qualitative, not a concentration. In addition, the set deliberately omits: - **Body fluid, CSF specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports an IL-6 value: `3023091 / 26881-3 — Interleukin 6 [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source reports IL-6 from CSF or body fluid**: do **not** map. Out of scope. - **Source reports a qualitative result (positive/negative)**: do **not** map. - **Do not confuse with**: other interleukins (IL-1, IL-8, IL-10 — different cytokines), procalcitonin (PCT, separate concept set), C reactive protein (CRP, separate concept set). ## References [1] Tanaka T, Narazaki M, Kishimoto T. IL-6 in inflammation, immunity, and disease. *Cold Spring Harb Perspect Biol*. 2014;6(10):a016295. Available: https://doi.org/10.1101/cshperspect.a016295 [2] Hay KA, Hanafi LA, Li D, Gust J, Liles WC, Wurfel MM, et al. Kinetics and biomarkers of severe cytokine release syndrome after CD19 chimeric antigen receptor-modified T-cell therapy. *Blood*. 2017;130(21):2295-2306. Available: https://doi.org/10.1182/blood-2017-06-793141 [3] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 26881-3 — Interleukin 6 [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/26881-3/
